summary.title: New Threat Model
summary.description:
This system is an AWS-based content-research and AI document-processing platform called “CRP” . It is designed to accept traffic through a public ingress layer, protected by a WAF and load balancer, and route requests into backend application services running on ECS Fargate.

At the application layer, the platform uses an ECS cluster named `crp-platform` with several containerized services :
- `backend-orchestrator` as the main coordination/API service 
- `extraction-service` for document extraction tasks 
- `research-agent` for research-related processing 
- `ingestion-worker` for ingestion/background job handling 

The backend orchestrates work across the internal services, forming a pipeline where incoming requests trigger extraction, research, and worker processes.

For storage, the platform uses multiple AWS-managed data services:
- PostgreSQL RDS (`crp-case-db`) for structured case data, with storage encryption enabled 
- S3 bucket (`crp-ingested-documents`) for document storage, protected with KMS-backed server-side encryption 
- AWS Secrets Manager (`crp/app-secrets`) for sensitive application secrets, also protected with KMS 

The architecture also integrates private AI capabilities through VPC endpoints, specifically for services like Textract and Bedrock, which suggests support for OCR and LLM-based document analysis without exposing those calls over the public internet.

In short, this is a cloud-native, microservice-style AWS platform for ingesting, processing, researching, and storing documents and case data, with encrypted storage, secret management, and private AI-service integration.
